code: @Manual{,   title = {categoricalvariabledistribution: Provide distributions of categorical variable value sets},   author = {Hanieh Razzaghi and Kaleigh Wieand and Kimberley Dickinson and Charles Bailey},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/ssdqa/categoricalvariabledistribution}, }

what: Provide distributions of categorical variable value sets
title: Provide distributions of categorical variable value sets
text: Categorical Variable Distribution module assesses presence frequency categorical values column dataset. example, can display spectrum units associated lab medication.

title: Provide distributions of categorical variable value sets
text: can install development version package like :

title: CVD Sample Concept Set — cvd_concept_set
text: sample version table structure expected concept_set parameter cvd_process function. user recreate file include clinical concepts interest.

title: CVD Sample Concept Set — cvd_concept_set
CVD Sample Concept Set — cvd_concept_set
text: object class tbl_df (inherits tbl, data.frame) 2 rows 4 columns.

title: CVD Sample Concept Set — cvd_concept_set
text: @format ## cvd_concept_set data frame 4 columns: concept_id OMOP concept_id; PCORnet CDM used, default column random integer like row number concept_name (optional)string name concept concept_code original code associated concept_id vocabulary_id vocabulary associated concept; PCORnet CDM used, ensure values field match vocabulary abbreviations used CDM

title: CVD Sample Domain Table — cvd_domain_tbl
text: sample version table structure expected domain_tbl parameter cvd_process function. user recreate table include domain definitions.

title: CVD Sample Domain Table — cvd_domain_tbl
CVD Sample Domain Table — cvd_domain_tbl
text: object class tbl_df (inherits tbl, data.frame) 1 rows 4 columns.

title: CVD Sample Domain Table — cvd_domain_tbl
text: #' @format ## cvd_domain_tbl domain name CDM table associated domain interest concept_field name column domain table contains concepts interest listed concept_set file. vs_field name column domain table show distribution categorical values. date_field name column domain table contains dates used time-based filtering.

title: Multi Site, Anomaly, Cross-Sectional Output for Categorical Variable Distribution Module — cvd_ms_anom_cs
text: dot plot, returned ggplot object, site x axis, categorical valueset item y axis, dot size representing mean proportion given valueset item across sites, color representing proportion given site's values given valueset item. Anomalies distinguished star shapes opposed normal circles.

title: Multi Site, Anomaly, Longitudinal Output for Categorical Variable Distribution Module — cvd_ms_anom_la

what: Categorical Variable Distribution - Output Generation — cvd_output
title: Categorical Variable Distribution - Output Generation — cvd_output
text: Using tabular output generated cvd_process, function build visualization results function configuration output bespoke ggplot object. Plot theming can adjusted user graph bee output using + theme(). Plots can also made interactive using make_interactive_squba() object output cvd_output.

title: Categorical Variable Distribution - Output Generation — cvd_output
Categorical Variable Distribution - Output Generation — cvd_output
text: process_output tabular output | output cvd_process filter_concept numeric/string vector | specific code(s) focus analysis; used ss_anom_la ms_anom_la vocab_tbl tabular input | OPTIONAL: location external vocabulary table containing concept names provided codes. NULL, concept names available either reference table hover tooltip

title: Categorical Variable Distribution - Output Generation — cvd_output
text: graph table visualize results

title: Categorical Variable Distribution - Output Generation — cvd_output
code: #' Source setup file source(system.file('setup.R', package = 'categoricalvariabledistribution'))  #' Create in-memory RSQLite database using data in extdata directory conn <- mk_testdb_omop()  #' Establish connection to database and generate internal configurations initialize_dq_session(session_name = 'cvd_process_test',                       working_directory = getwd(),                       db_conn = conn,                       is_json = FALSE,                       file_subdirectory = 'extdata',                       cdm_schema = NA) #> Connected to: :memory:@NA  #' Build mock study cohort cohort_tbl <- cdm_tbl('person') %>% dplyr::distinct(person_id) %>%   dplyr::mutate(start_date = as.Date(-5000), # RSQLite does not store date objects,                 # hence the numerics                 end_date = as.Date(15000),                 site = ifelse(person_id %in% c(1:6), 'synth1', 'synth2'))  #' Execute `cvd_process` function #' This example will use the single site, exploratory, cross sectional #' configuration cvd_process_example <- cvd_process(cohort=cohort_tbl,                                   domain_tbl=categoricalvariabledistribution::cvd_domain_tbl,                                   concept_set=categoricalvariabledistribution::cvd_concept_set,                                   omop_or_pcornet='omop',                                   multi_or_single_site = 'single',                                   anomaly_or_exploratory='exploratory',                                   time = FALSE) #> Rows: 77 Columns: 5 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (5): module, check, Always Required, Required for Check, Optional #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. #> Joining with `by = join_by(person_id, start_date, end_date, site, site_summ, fu_diff, fu)` #> Error in db_query_fields.DBIConnection(con, ...): Can't query fields. #> ℹ Using SQL: SELECT * FROM `measurement` AS `q01` WHERE (0 = 1) #> Caused by error: #> ! no such table: measurement  cvd_process_example #> Error: object 'cvd_process_example' not found  #' Execute `cvd_output` function #' The output was edited for a better indication of what the visualization will #' look like. #' The 0s are a limitation of the small sample data set used for this example cvd_output_example <- cvd_output(process_output = cvd_process_example) #> Error: object 'cvd_process_example' not found  cvd_output_example #> Error: object 'cvd_output_example' not found  #' Easily convert the graph into an interactive ggiraph or plotly object with #' `make_interactive_squba()`  make_interactive_squba(cvd_output_example) #> Error: object 'cvd_output_example' not found

what: Categorical Variable Distribution Module Processing — cvd_process
title: Categorical Variable Distribution Module Processing — cvd_process
text: module takes concept set field interest input creates distribution categorical values field interest. example, user can input drug concept set get back distribution units associated drug, user can input lab test concept set get back distribution units associated lab results.

title: Categorical Variable Distribution Module Processing — cvd_process
code: cvd_process(   cohort,   domain_tbl = NULL,   concept_set = NULL,   omop_or_pcornet,   multi_or_single_site = "single",   anomaly_or_exploratory = "exploratory",   p_value = 0.9,   time = FALSE,   time_span = c("2012-01-01", "2020-01-01"),   time_period = "year" )

title: Categorical Variable Distribution Module Processing — cvd_process
text: cohort tabular input | dataframe cohort patients study. include columns: person_id / patid | integer / character start_date | date end_date | date site | character domain_tbl tabular input | table domain definitions. example file provided categoricalvariabledistribution::cvd_domain_file. include columns: domain | name CDM table associated domain interest concept_field | name column domain table contains concepts interest listed concept_set file vs_field | name column domain table show distribution categorical values. date_field | name column domain table contains dates used time-based filtering concept_set tabular input | annotated concept set following columns: concept_id | integer |  required OMOP; concept_id interest concept_name | character | optional; descriptive name concept concept_code | character | required PCORnet; code interest vocabulary_id | character | required PCORnet; vocabulary code - match listed domain table's vocabulary_field omop_or_pcornet string | Option run function using OMOP PCORnet CDM default CDM omop: run cvd_process_omop() function OMOP CDM instance pcornet: run cvd_process_pcornet() function PCORnet CDM instance multi_or_single_site string | Option run function single vs multiple sites single: run function single site multi: run function multiple sites anomaly_or_exploratory string | Option conduct exploratory anomaly detection analysis. Exploratory analyses give high level summary data examine fact representation within cohort. Anomaly detection analyses specialized identify outliers within cohort. p_value numeric | p value used threshold multi-site anomaly detection analysis time boolean | logical determine whether output check across time time_span vector - length 2 | time = TRUE, vector two dates observation period study time_period string | time = TRUE, argument defines distance dates within specified time period. Defaults year, time periods month week also acceptable

title: Categorical Variable Distribution Module Processing — cvd_process
text: data frame summary results can used cvd_output generate graphical tabular output

title: Categorical Variable Distribution Module Processing — cvd_process
code: #' Source setup file source(system.file('setup.R', package = 'categoricalvariabledistribution'))  #' Create in-memory RSQLite database using data in extdata directory conn <- mk_testdb_omop()  #' Establish connection to database and generate internal configurations initialize_dq_session(session_name = 'cvd_process_test',                       working_directory = getwd(),                       db_conn = conn,                       is_json = FALSE,                       file_subdirectory = 'extdata',                       cdm_schema = NA) #> Connected to: :memory:@NA  #' Build mock study cohort cohort_tbl <- cdm_tbl('person') %>% dplyr::distinct(person_id) %>%   dplyr::mutate(start_date = as.Date(-5000), # RSQLite does not store date objects,                 # hence the numerics                 end_date = as.Date(15000),                 site = ifelse(person_id %in% c(1:6), 'synth1', 'synth2'))  #' Execute `cvd_process` function #' This example will use the single site, exploratory, cross sectional #' configuration cvd_process_example <- cvd_process(cohort=cohort_tbl,                                   domain_tbl=categoricalvariabledistribution::cvd_domain_tbl,                                   concept_set=categoricalvariabledistribution::cvd_concept_set,                                   omop_or_pcornet='omop',                                   multi_or_single_site = 'single',                                   anomaly_or_exploratory='exploratory',                                   time = FALSE) #> Rows: 77 Columns: 5 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (5): module, check, Always Required, Required for Check, Optional #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. #> Joining with `by = join_by(person_id, start_date, end_date, site, site_summ, fu_diff, fu)` #> Error in db_query_fields.DBIConnection(con, ...): Can't query fields. #> ℹ Using SQL: SELECT * FROM `measurement` AS `q01` WHERE (0 = 1) #> Caused by error: #> ! no such table: measurement  cvd_process_example #> Error: object 'cvd_process_example' not found  #' Execute `cvd_output` function #' The output was edited for a better indication of what the visualization will #' look like. #' The 0s are a limitation of the small sample data set used for this example cvd_output_example <- cvd_output(process_output = cvd_process_example) #> Error: object 'cvd_process_example' not found  cvd_output_example #> Error: object 'cvd_output_example' not found  #' Easily convert the graph into an interactive ggiraph or plotly object with #' `make_interactive_squba()`  make_interactive_squba(cvd_output_example) #> Error: object 'cvd_output_example' not found

title: Categorical Variable Distribution - OMOP Version — cvd_process_omop
text: cohort tabular input | dataframe cohort patients study. include columns: person_id / patid | integer / character start_date | date end_date | date site | character domain_tbl tabular input | table domain definitions. example file provided categoricalvariabledistribution::cvd_domain_file. include columns: domain | name CDM table associated domain interest concept_field | name column domain table contains concepts interest listed concept_set file vs_field | name column domain table show distribution categorical values. date_field | name column domain table contains dates used time-based filtering concept_set tabular input | annotated concept set following columns: concept_id | integer |  required OMOP; concept_id interest concept_name | character | optional; descriptive name concept concept_code | character | required PCORnet; code interest vocabulary_id | character | required PCORnet; vocabulary code - match listed domain table's vocabulary_field multi_or_single_site string | Option run function single vs multiple sites single: run function single site multi: run function multiple sites anomaly_or_exploratory string | Option conduct exploratory anomaly detection analysis. Exploratory analyses give high level summary data examine fact representation within cohort. Anomaly detection analyses specialized identify outliers within cohort. p_value numeric | p value used threshold multi-site anomaly detection analysis time boolean | logical determine whether output check across time time_span vector - length 2 | time = TRUE, vector two dates observation period study time_period string | time = TRUE, argument defines distance dates within specified time period. Defaults year, time periods month week also acceptable

text: analysis executed year greater, P Prime control chart returned outliers marked orange dots
code: if analysis was executed by month or smaller, an STL regression is     conducted and outliers are marked with red dots. the graphs representing     the data removed in the regression are also returned
